question: Change to Indirect: She said to me, 'I am writing a letter to your brother.'
options: She told me that she was writing a letter to my brother.
She told me that I was writing a letter to your brother.
She told me that she is writing a letter to my brother.
She said to me that she was writing a letter to her brother.
answer: She told me that she was writing a letter to my brother.

question: Change to Indirect: He said to them, 'You have completed your task.'
options: He told them that they have completed their task.
He told them that you had completed your task.
He told them that they had completed their task.
He told them that he had completed his task.
answer: He told them that they had completed their task.
explanation: Second person 'You' and 'your' change according to the object 'them' ➔ 'they' and 'their'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: I said to her, 'I gave you my book yesterday.'
options: I told her that I had given her my book the previous day.
I told her that she had given me her book the day before.
I told her that I gave her my book the day before.
I told her that I had given you my book yesterday.
answer: I told her that I had given her my book the previous day.
explanation: Simple Past (gave) ➔ Past Perfect (had given). 'yesterday' ➔ 'the previous day'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: The boy said, 'I shall go to Delhi tomorrow.'
options: The boy said that he shall go to Delhi the next day.
The boy said that he would go to Delhi tomorrow.
The boy said that he would go to Delhi the next day.
The boy said that I would go to Delhi the following day.
answer: The boy said that he would go to Delhi the next day.
explanation: 'Shall/Will' usually changes to 'would' in indirect speech, and 'tomorrow' ➔ 'the next day'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: She said, 'These books are mine.'
options: She said that these books were hers.
She said that those books were hers.
She said that those books are hers.
She said that these books were mine.
answer: She said that those books were hers.

question: Change to Indirect: He said, 'I was here last week.'
options: He said that he was there last week.
He said that he had been there the previous week.
He said that he had been here the previous week.
He said that he has been there the week before.
answer: He said that he had been there the previous week.
explanation: 'was' (Past) ➔ 'had been' (Past Perfect). 'here' ➔ 'there' and 'last week' ➔ 'the previous week'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: They said to us, 'We cannot live without water.'
options: They told us that they could not live without water.
They told us that we cannot live without water.
They told us that they cannot live without water.
Both B and C are correct.
answer: Both B and C are correct.
explanation: Since 'living without water' is a universal fact, the tense remains unchanged. Also, 'we' can refer to humanity in general. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: Ram said, 'I have been staying in this room for ten days.'
options: Ram said that he had been staying in that room for ten days.
Ram said that he has been staying in this room for ten days.
Ram said that he had been staying in this room for ten days.
Ram said that I had been staying in that room for ten days.
answer: Ram said that he had been staying in that room for ten days.
explanation: Present Perfect Continuous ➔ Past Perfect Continuous, and 'this' ➔ 'that'. ✅

question: Change to Indirect: The old man said, 'I am very tired now.'
options: The old man said that he was very tired then.
The old man said that I was very tired then.
The old man said that he is very tired now.
The old man told that he was very tired then.
answer: The old man said that he was very tired then.

question: Change to Indirect: She said to her friend, 'I will call you later today.'
options: She told her friend that she would call her later today.
She told her friend that she would call her later that day.
She told her friend that she will call her later that day.
She said to her friend that she would call her later that day.
answer: She told her friend that she would call her later that day.
